title: Testing targets.utils
text: Tests skip gracefully optional dependencies missing RStudio addin tests skip non-interactive environments Allows CI/CD run without RStudio installed

title: Testing targets.utils
text: Uses testthat’s clear assertion functions (expect_true(), expect_equal(), etc.) Descriptive test names Comments explaining complex test logic

title: Testing targets.utils
text: test suite designed work continuous integration environments: Tests : - Skip RStudio-dependent tests CI environments - Skip tests missing optional dependencies - Exit appropriate code failure
code: # Example GitHub Actions workflow - name: Run tests   run: devtools::test()   shell: Rscript {0}

title: Testing targets.utils
code: test_that("function_name() does something", {   skip_if_not_installed("required_package")      # Setup   input <- create_test_input()      # Execute   result <- function_name(input)      # Assert   expect_equal(result, expected_value) })

title: Testing targets.utils
text: Descriptive names: Use test_that("description", ...) clear descriptions Setup-Execute-Assert: Follow 3A pattern One assertion per test: Prefer multiple focused tests one test many assertions Skip unavailable dependencies: Use skip_if_not_installed() Isolate side effects: Use tempfile() file system operations Clean : Ensure temporary files deleted (e.g., unlink())

what: Example: New Test for a Function
title: Testing targets.utils
code: test_that("my_function() handles edge case correctly", {   skip_if_not_installed("required_package")      # Setup with temporary directory   td <- tempfile()   dir.create(td)      withr::with_dir(td, {     # Test code here     result <- my_function(test_input)     expect_equal(result, expected_value)   }) })

code: tar_target(    name = ,    command =   ), tar_script({   list(     tar_target(name = raw_data, command = read.csv("data.csv")),          # Position cursor here and slam shortcut key to insert the next target skeleton          tar_target(name = next_data, command = read.csv("next_data.csv"))   ) })

what: Is this the Pompidou? I am lost in the pipeline
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: Navigate pipeline dependencies tar_downstream() tar_upstream()
code: # See what depends on the 'raw_data' target tar_downstream(raw_data) # [1] "processed_data" "summary_stats" "final_model" ...  # See only direct dependents (immediate = TRUE) tar_downstream(raw_data, immediate = TRUE) # [1] "processed_data" "summary_stats"  # See what 'final_model' depends on tar_upstream(final_model) # [1] "raw_data" "processed_data" "model_config"  # See only direct dependencies tar_upstream(final_model, immediate = TRUE) # [1] "processed_data" "model_config"  # Useful for understanding impact of changes and tracing dependencies

what: My data structures are overcomplicated and fussy: spatial edition
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: Handling nested spatial data tar_terra_nested() fucking excellent geotargets package handles terra::SpatRaster terra::SpatVector objects, chokes lists containing nested spatial objects. tar_terra_nested() fills gap recursively finding managing nested spatial formats buried inside arbitrary R objects. recursive? arbitrary? Try . ’ll probably break.

previous_headings: My data structures are overcomplicated and fussy: spatial edition
what: Example: Nested spatial data in a list
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
code: library(targets)  tar_dir({    # tar_dir() runs code from a temporary directory.    tar_script({      library(targets)     library(geotargets)     library(terra)     library(tibble)     library(targets.utils)      # Create a function that produces a nested object with spatial data     make_nested_object <- function() {        r <- terra::rast(         system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra")       )        v <- terra::vect(         system.file("ex", "lux.shp", package = "terra")       )        # Return a list with nested SpatRaster and SpatVector objects       list(         raster = r,         vector = v,         metadata = tibble(           dataset = "example",           n_cells = terra::ncell(r),           n_features = nrow(v)         )       )     }      list(       # Use tar_terra_nested() just like tar_target() to handle the list       tar_terra_nested(         nested_spatial,         make_nested_object()       )     )   })    tar_make()    # Load and inspect the nested spatial object   x <- tar_read(nested_spatial)   x  }) #> terra 1.9.38 #> + nested_spatial dispatched #> ✔ nested_spatial completed [12ms, 46.45 kB] #> ✔ ended pipeline [214ms, 1 completed, 0 skipped] #> $raster #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 90, 95, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.008333333, 0.008333333  (x, y) #> extent      : 5.741667, 6.533333, 49.44167, 50.19167  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> source      : rast_000001.tif #> name        : elevation #> min value   :       141 #> max value   :       547 #>  #> $vector #> class       : SpatVector #> geometry    : polygons #> dimensions  : 12, 6  (geometries, attributes) #> extent      : 5.74414, 6.528252, 49.44781, 50.18162  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> source      : vect_000002.gpkg #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> names       :  ID_1   NAME_1  ID_2   NAME_2  AREA   POP #> type        : <num>    <chr> <num>    <chr> <num> <num> #> values      :     1 Diekirch     1 Clervaux   312 18081 #>                   1 Diekirch     2 Diekirch   218 32543 #>                   1 Diekirch     3  Redange   259 18664 #>               ... #>  #> $metadata #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   dataset n_cells n_features #> * <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 example    8550         12

previous_headings: My data structures are overcomplicated and fussy: spatial edition
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: targets package stores objects RDS files default, serialize terra::SpatRaster terra::SpatVector objects. geotargets package solves top-level spatial objects custom formats write GeoTIFF GeoPackage. geotargets can’t handle objects contain nested spatial formats, : Lists spatial elements Data frames spatial list-columns Nested lists mixing spatial non-spatial data S4 objects spatial slot data tar_terra_nested() works : Recursively scanning object SpatRaster SpatVector objects Writing individual files (GeoTIFF rasters, GeoPackage vectors) Replacing lightweight reference objects skeleton Compressing skeleton + spatial files single .tar.gz archive Reconstructing object restoring spatial files read

previous_headings: My data structures are overcomplicated and fussy: spatial edition
what: Advanced: Custom file formats and options
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: can customize spatial data written: AI much donkeywork probably break. Trust peril.
code: tar_script({   list(     tar_terra_nested(       my_target,       make_complex_nested_object(),       # Use Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF for rasters       raster_filetype = "COG",       # Custom raster creation options       raster_gdal = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "PREDICTOR=2"),       # Use shapefile instead of GeoPackage for vectors       vector_filetype = "ESRI SHAPEFILE",       # Pass additional options to terra functions       raster_args = list(NAflag = -9999),       vector_args = list(layer = "myshapes")     )   ) })

previous_headings: My data structures are overcomplicated and fussy: spatial edition
what: Example: Nested spatial data in a tibble
title: Simple utilities for working in targets
text: can even use tar_terra_nested() tibbles containing spatial list-columns:
code: tar_script({   library(targets)   library(terra)   library(tibble)   library(targets.utils)    make_spatial_tibble <- function() {     r1 <- terra::rast(system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra"))     r2 <- terra::rast(system.file("ex", "elev.tif", package = "terra"))          tibble(       id = c("location_a", "location_b"),       raster = list(r1, r2),       description = c("Elevation at A", "Elevation at B")     )   }    list(     tar_terra_nested(       spatial_tibble,       make_spatial_tibble()     )   ) })

what: Get downstream targets from a specified target — tar_downstream

text: Find targets depend given target, either directly indirectly. useful understanding impact changes target rest pipeline.

title: Get downstream targets from a specified target — tar_downstream
code: tar_downstream(target_name, immediate = FALSE)

title: Get downstream targets from a specified target — tar_downstream
text: target_name Symbol character. Name target find downstream targets . immediate Logical. `TRUE`, return first-degree dependencies (targets directly depend `target_name`). `FALSE` (default), return downstream targets, including indirect dependencies.

title: Get downstream targets from a specified target — tar_downstream
text: Character vector downstream target names (excluding target   ). Returns empty character vector downstream   targets.

title: Get downstream targets from a specified target — tar_downstream
text: function requires `igraph` package, listed Imports. queries current targets pipeline via `targets::tar_network()` traverses dependency graph find targets depend specified target.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After tar_make(), show what depends on 'raw_data' tar_downstream(raw_data)  # Only direct dependents tar_downstream("processed_data", immediate = TRUE) } # }

what: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: format recursively searches R object nested [terra::SpatRaster-class] [terra::SpatVector-class] objects.

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
code: tar_format_terra_nested(   raster_filetype = "GTiff",   raster_gdal = NULL,   raster_datatype = NULL,   vector_filetype = "GPKG",   vector_gdal = NULL,   raster_args = list(),   vector_args = list() )

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: raster_filetype Character length 1. GDAL raster driver passed [terra::writeRaster()]. Defaults `"GTiff"`. raster_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL raster creation options passed [terra::writeRaster()] `gdal` argument. raster_datatype Character length 1 `NULL`. Optional datatype passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_filetype Character length 1. GDAL vector driver passed [terra::writeVector()]. Defaults `"GPKG"`. vector_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL vector creation options passed [terra::writeVector()] `options` argument. raster_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeVector()].

title: Create a custom targets format for nested terra objects — tar_format_terra_nested
text: custom target format object [targets::tar_format()].

what: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
text: `tar_terra_nested()` target factory objects contain nested [terra::SpatRaster-class] [terra::SpatVector-class] objects top-level terra objects. Examples include lists, nested lists, tibbles list-columns, many ordinary serialisable R objects.

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
code: tar_terra_nested(   name,   command,   pattern = NULL,   raster_filetype = "GTiff",   raster_gdal = NULL,   raster_datatype = NULL,   vector_filetype = "GPKG",   vector_gdal = NULL,   raster_args = list(),   vector_args = list(),   packages = targets::tar_option_get("packages"),   library = targets::tar_option_get("library"),   repository = targets::tar_option_get("repository"),   error = targets::tar_option_get("error"),   memory = targets::tar_option_get("memory"),   garbage_collection = targets::tar_option_get("garbage_collection"),   deployment = targets::tar_option_get("deployment"),   priority = targets::tar_option_get("priority"),   resources = targets::tar_option_get("resources"),   storage = targets::tar_option_get("storage"),   retrieval = targets::tar_option_get("retrieval"),   cue = targets::tar_option_get("cue"),   description = targets::tar_option_get("description") )

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested
text: name Symbol. Name target. command R code returning object may contain nested `SpatRaster` /`SpatVector` objects. pattern Code define dynamic branching pattern. See [targets::tar_target()]. raster_filetype Character length 1. GDAL raster driver passed [terra::writeRaster()]. Defaults `"GTiff"`. raster_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL raster creation options passed [terra::writeRaster()] `gdal` argument. raster_datatype Character length 1 `NULL`. Optional datatype passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_filetype Character length 1. GDAL vector driver passed [terra::writeVector()]. Defaults `"GPKG"`. vector_gdal Character vector `NULL`. GDAL vector creation options passed [terra::writeVector()] `options` argument. raster_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeRaster()]. vector_args Named list additional arguments passed [terra::writeVector()]. packages Character vector packages load target. library Character vector library paths. repository Character length 1. Storage repository target. error Character length 1. Error handling strategy. memory Character length 1. Memory strategy. garbage_collection Logical. Whether run garbage collection. deployment Character length 1. Deployment strategy. priority Numeric length 1. Target priority. resources Object returned [targets::tar_resources()]. storage Character length 1. Storage strategy. retrieval Character length 1. Retrieval strategy. cue Object returned [targets::tar_cue()]. description Character length 1. Target description.

title: Create a target for nested objects containing terra objects — tar_terra_nested

GDAL raster options. raster_datatype Character NULL. Raster datatype. vector_filetype Character. GDAL vector driver. vector_gdal Character vector NULL.
